CERC: INTRODUCTION
Appendix: Accessible E
Figure 1-1. Crisis and Emergency Risk D
Communication (CERC) Rhythm: Crisis n
communication needs and activities evolve t
through four phases in every emergency. The in
first phase is preparation. During preparation r
communicators should draft and test messages, p
develop partnerships, create communication plans, d
and determine the approval process for sending out p
information in an emergency. The second phase is C
the initial phase. During the initial phase of a crisis c
communicators should express empathy, explain m
risks, promote action, and describe response efforts. c
Explanation of Figure
During the third phase, maintenance, communicators
need to explain ongoing risks and will have more
time to segment audiences, providing background
nformation, and addressing rumors. The final phase,
resolution, requires communicators to motivate the
public to stay vigilant and communicators should
discuss lessons learned and revise communication
plans for future emergencies. Throughout all phases,
CERC encourages communicators to engage
communities, empower community members to
make decisions that impact their health, and evaluate
communication efforts.
